mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and margin compression, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns at a frequency exceeding 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250, elevated inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL stays above 250.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 5-day S&P 500 direction, with accuracy below 52 percent over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable employment during a recovery phase indicates successful capacity utilization and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases fail to predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with statistical significance at p less than 0.05.
